chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly states in Section V and the Abstract that an open-source implementation is available on GitHub at https://github.com/lsd-ucsc/Decentagram. This satisfies the requirement for working links to public repositories.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation relies entirely on software simulations using Ganache (local Ethereum testing environment), Goerli testnet, and a PC running Ubuntu with Geth client. There is no mention of physical hardware deployment or large-scale cloud emulations beyond standard node setups.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: Section III ('SYSTEM AND THREAT MODEL') contains a dedicated section that formally defines the threat model, specifying assumptions about attacker control fractions (1/3 for availability, 1/2 for forks), TEE host control capabilities, and rationality of data oracles.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides rigorous, data-driven analysis in Section VII. It quantifies gas costs for deployment and operations (Table II), calculates monetary costs based on mainnet/L2 rates, measures off-chain throughput (blocks/second) against block arrival rates, and compares end-to-end latency between Decentagram and the M&R baseline.

major_weakness: The experimental evaluation is limited to standard software simulations (Ganache/Goerli) without physical hardware testbeds or massive-scale emulations.
